summary: Backend: 10/10 pytest tests pass (health, languages, validation, JPEG analyze in EN, ZH translation, PDFŌåÆPNG via PyMuPDF, list/get/delete/delete-all with device isolation). Frontend: onboarding ŌåÆ language picker (all 6 languages) ŌåÆ home (all CTAs/chips/empty state) ŌåÆ settings ŌåÆ change language to ZH (home re-renders in ń«ĆõĮōõĖŁµ¢ć) all work. CRITICAL: result screen crashes with 'Element type is invalid' because `MailReply` is imported from lucide-react-native but does not exist in that package (only `Reply` / `ReplyAll` are exported). This breaks navigation to ANY analysis result.

ui_bugs.issue: Imports `MailReply` from 'lucide-react-native' but this icon is not exported by the package. At runtime `MailReply` is `undefined`, so the first JSX render of the screen throws 'Element type is invalid: expected a string ... but got: undefined'. Entire result screen is unusable, including from history list and direct deep link.

integration_issues.issue: Backend POST /api/analyze returns a complete record correctly, but the frontend cannot display it because result.tsx fails to render due to the missing `MailReply` icon import.

action_items: FIX result.tsx: replace `MailReply` import with a valid lucide-react-native icon, e.g. `Reply` (or `MessageSquareReply`, `CornerUpLeft`). Update the `<MailReply ... />` usage in the German reply draft section accordingly.

After the fix, re-render /result?id=<existing id> with seeded device_id 92f12911-efce-4b72-b0ae-a2b5d8a39af7 (record id b0da98a1-d461-4762-9726-3525568f6a0a is in DB) and confirm all cards render without console errors.
Recommend a quick smoke check of all icon imports in result.tsx, history.tsx, scan.tsx, upload.tsx, analyzing.tsx against lucide-react-native to avoid similar undefined-icon crashes.
critical_code_review_comments: Frontend has multiple Expo SDK package version mismatches reported by Expo (async-storage, expo-camera, expo-clipboard, expo-document-picker, expo-file-system, expo-image-picker, react-native-svg). Not blocking web preview but may cause native build issues.
Backend cleanly excludes MongoDB `_id` in all responses (verified). Good.
PyMuPDF PDFŌåÆPNG conversion working end-to-end (verified with synthetic German PDF).
GPT-5.2 returns valid structured JSON; ZH responses are non-ASCII (CJK) as expected.
seed_data_creation: Seeded one analysis for device_id=klarpost-mvp (id=klarpost-mvp, target_language=zh, risk=red, AOK Bayern Mahnung) so the home screen shows a recent-analyses card and the result screen can be tested directly after the icon fix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_klarpost_api.py is comprehensive and reusable. After main agent fixes the MailReply icon import, retest /result?id=klarpost-mvp using localStorage seed { 'klarpost.deviceId':'92f12911-efce-4b72-b0ae-a2b5d8a39af7', 'klarpost.onboarded':'1', 'klarpost.language':'zh' } to verify all cards render. Also test reply-copy button behavior.
rca_of_issue: Root cause: `import { ..., MailReply, ... } from 'lucide-react-native'` in /app/frontend/app/result.tsx line 27. The lucide-react-native package (v0.x in this project) does NOT export an icon named `MailReply` (verified by grepping lucide-react-native/dist/lucide-react-native.d.ts ŌĆö only Reply, ReplyAll exist). Therefore `MailReply` is `undefined` at runtime, and React throws 'Element type is invalid: expected a string ... but got: undefined' the moment the result screen renders the SectionRow that uses `<MailReply />`. Reproduction: navigate to /result?id=<any> with a valid record. Fix: change `MailReply` to `Reply` (closest semantic match) in both the import list and the `<SectionRow icon={<MailReply ... />} ... />` usage in result.tsx.
